title: Canary30 Causality Confirmation: Chunk Parameter Revert Analysis

executive_summary.conclusion: CAUSALITY CONFIRMED - Chunk parameter changes (150/5 → 200/8) caused the -25% Recall@5 regression
executive_summary.evidence: Revert to 150/5 parameters shows IDENTICAL metrics to regression run (200/8), indicating the regression is NOT caused by parameters but by corpus/embedding state
executive_summary.status: INVESTIGATION REQUIRED - Root cause is NOT the parameters themselves

finding_1_parameters_not_root_cause.title: Parameters Are NOT the Root Cause
evidence: Regression run (200/8) and revert run (150/5) show IDENTICAL recall metrics
Per-difficulty breakdown identical across both runs
finding_1_parameters_not_root_cause.implication: The -25% Recall@5 regression vs 2026-04-27 baseline is NOT caused by chunk parameter changes

finding_2_corpus_or_embedding_state.title: Root Cause: Corpus State or Embedding Cache
evidence: Both 150/5 and 200/8 show same poor performance
Suggests corpus was already in degraded state before parameter change
Embedding cache may be stale or corrupted
Corpus size change (11449 → 11457 chunks) may have invalidated embeddings
finding_2_corpus_or_embedding_state.hypothesis: Embedding cache was not properly rebuilt after corpus modification

finding_3_latency_variance.title: Latency Shows Expected Variance
evidence: Revert run avg latency: 1124.55ms (+5.1% vs regression run)
Variance is within expected range for same configuration
finding_3_latency_variance.implication: Latency differences are noise, not systematic

finding_4_difficulty_breakdown_consisten.title: Difficulty Breakdown Identical
evidence: Hard queries: 0% recall@5 in both runs
Medium queries: 80% recall@5 in both runs
Simple queries: 38.89% recall@5 in both runs
finding_4_difficulty_breakdown_consisten.implication: Retrieval quality is consistently poor across both parameter sets

causality_conclusion.statement: CAUSALITY NOT CONFIRMED - Parameters did NOT cause the regression
reasoning: If parameters caused regression, reverting should improve metrics
Metrics are IDENTICAL between 200/8 and 150/5 runs
Therefore, parameters are not the causal factor
Root cause must be elsewhere: embedding cache, corpus state, or retrieval pipeline
causality_conclusion.severity: HIGH - Regression persists even after parameter revert

immediate: Clear embedding cache and rebuild from scratch
Verify corpus integrity (chunk count, content hash)
Check if 2026-04-27 baseline used different embedding model or cache state
investigation: Compare embedding vectors between 2026-04-27 and 2026-05-03 runs
Check retrieval pipeline logs for errors or warnings
Verify query expansion and contextual chunking are disabled (as configured)
Profile per-query results to identify which queries regressed most
validation: Run full 109-paper evaluation to confirm canary30 findings generalize
Compare against 2026-04-27 baseline with same parameters (150/5)
If baseline also shows regression, confirms corpus/cache issue
If baseline shows 0.6667 recall@5, confirms cache invalidation

do_not: Do NOT revert chunk parameters - they are not the cause
Do NOT adjust parameters further until root cause is identified
Do NOT assume parameters caused regression without cache investigation
do: Verify corpus state and chunk integrity
Re-run 2026-04-27 baseline with current code to confirm cache issue
